89	        self.dummy_req = Req(
90	            input_ids=torch.tensor([0], dtype=torch.int32, device="cpu"),
91	            table_idx=config.max_running_req,
92	            cached_len=0,
93	            output_len=1,
94	            uid=-1,
95	            sampling_params=None,  # type: ignore
96	            cache_handle=None,  # type: ignore
97	        )
98	        self.page_table[self.dummy_req.table_idx].fill_(num_tokens)  # point to dummy page
99	        self.graph_runner = GraphRunner(
100	            stream=self.stream,
101	            device=self.device,
102	            model=self.model,
103	            attn_backend=self.attn_backend,
104	            cuda_graph_bs=config.cuda_graph_bs,
105	            cuda_graph_max_bs=config.cuda_graph_max_bs,
106	            free_memory=init_free_memory,
107	            max_seq_len=aligned_max_seq_len,
108	            vocab_size=config.model_config.vocab_size,
109	            dummy_req=self.dummy_req,
110	        )
111	
112	    def _init_communication(self, config: EngineConfig) -> torch.distributed.ProcessGroup:
113	        if config.tp_info.size == 1 or config.use_pynccl:
114	            torch.distributed.init_process_group(
115	                backend="gloo",
116	                rank=config.tp_info.rank,
117	                world_size=config.tp_info.size,
118	                timeout=timedelta(seconds=config.distributed_timeout),
119	                init_method=config.distributed_addr,
120	            )
121	            tp_cpu_group = torch.distributed.group.WORLD
122	            assert tp_cpu_group is not None
123	            max_bytes = (
124	                config.max_forward_len * config.model_config.hidden_size * self.dtype.itemsize
125	            )
126	            enable_pynccl_distributed(config.tp_info, tp_cpu_group, max_bytes)
127	        else:
128	            torch.distributed.init_process_group(
129	                backend="nccl",
130	                rank=config.tp_info.rank,
131	                world_size=config.tp_info.size,
132	                timeout=timedelta(seconds=config.distributed_timeout),
133	                init_method=config.distributed_addr,
134	            )
135	            tp_cpu_group = torch.distributed.new_group(backend="gloo")
136	            assert tp_cpu_group is not None
137	        return tp_cpu_group
138	
139	    def _load_weight_state_dict(self, config: EngineConfig) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
140	        if config.use_dummy_weight:
141	            return {
142	                k: torch.randn_like(v, device=self.device)
143	                for k, v in self.model.state_dict().items()
144	            }
145	        else:
146	            return {k: v.to(self.dtype) for k, v in load_weight(config.model_path, self.device)}
147	
148	    def _determine_num_pages(self, old_free_memory: int, config: EngineConfig) -> int:
149	        new_free_memory = self._sync_get_memory()[1]
150	        cache_per_page = (
151	            2  # key + value
152	            * config.model_config.head_dim
153	            * div_even(config.model_config.num_kv_heads, config.tp_info.size, allow_replicate=True)
154	            * config.page_size
155	            * self.dtype.itemsize
156	            * config.model_config.num_layers
157	        )
158	        num_pages = config.num_page_override
159	        if num_pages is None:
160	            model_memory = old_free_memory - new_free_memory
161	            available_memory = int(config.memory_ratio * old_free_memory) - model_memory
162	            num_pages = available_memory // cache_per_page
163	
164	        assert num_pages > 1, "Not enough memory for KV cache, try reducing --num-pages"
165	        num_tokens = num_pages * config.page_size
166	        real_kv_size = num_pages * cache_per_page
167	        logger.info(f"Allocating {num_tokens} tokens for KV cache, K + V = {mem_GB(real_kv_size)}")
168	        return num_pages
169	
170	    def _sync_get_memory(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
171	        """Get the min and max free memory across TP ranks."""
172	        torch.cuda.synchronize(self.device)
173	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
174	        torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats(self.device)
175	        free_memory = get_free_memory(self.device)
176	        free_mem_tensor = torch.tensor([free_memory, -free_memory], device="cpu", dtype=torch.int64)
177	        torch.distributed.all_reduce(
178	            free_mem_tensor, op=torch.distributed.ReduceOp.MIN, group=self.tp_cpu_group
179	        )
180	        min_free_memory = int(free_mem_tensor[0].item())
181	        max_free_memory = -int(free_mem_tensor[1].item())
182	        if max_free_memory - min_free_memory > 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024:
183	            logger.error(
184	                f"Memory across TP ranks are imbalanced:"
185	                f" min {mem_GB(min_free_memory)}, max {mem_GB(max_free_memory)}"
186	            )
187	            raise RuntimeError("Memory across TP ranks are imbalanced")
188	
189	        return min_free_memory, max_free_memory
190	
191	    def forward_batch(self, batch: Batch, args: BatchSamplingArgs) -> ForwardOutput:
192	        assert torch.cuda.current_stream() == self.stream
193	        with self.ctx.forward_batch(batch):
194	            if self.graph_runner.can_use_cuda_graph(batch):
195	                logits = self.graph_runner.replay(batch)
196	            else:
197	                logits = self.model.forward()
198	
199	        for req in batch.reqs:
200	            req.complete_one()
201	
202	        next_tokens_gpu = self.sampler.sample(logits[: batch.size], args).to(torch.int32)
203	        next_tokens_cpu = next_tokens_gpu.to("cpu", non_blocking=True)
204	        copy_done_event = torch.cuda.Event()
205	        copy_done_event.record(self.stream)
206	        return ForwardOutput(next_tokens_gpu, next_tokens_cpu, copy_done_event)
207	
208	    def shutdown(self) -> None:
209	        self.graph_runner.destroy_cuda_graphs()
210	        torch.distributed.destroy_process_group()
211	        destroy_distributed()
212	
213	
214	def _align_up_32(num: int) -> int:
215	    return (num + 31) // 32 * 32
216	
217	
218	def _adjust_config(config: EngineConfig):
219	    def override(attr: str, value: Any):  # this is dangerous, use with caution
220	        object.__setattr__(config, attr, value)
221	
222	    if config.attention_backend == "auto":
223	        backend = "trtllm" if is_sm100_supported() else ("fa,fi" if is_sm90_supported() else "fi")
224	        override("attention_backend", backend)
225	        logger.info_rank0(f"Auto-selected attention backend: {config.attention_backend}")
226	
227	    if "trtllm" in config.attention_backend and config.page_size not in [16, 32, 64]:
228	        override("page_size", 64)
229	        logger.warning_rank0("Page size is overridden to 64 for TRTLLM backend")
230	
231	    if config.model_config.is_moe and config.moe_backend == "auto":
232	        override("moe_backend", "fused")
233	        logger.info_rank0(f"Auto-selected MoE backend: {config.moe_backend}")
234

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import timedelta
from typing import Any, Dict, NamedTuple, Tuple

import torch
from minisgl.attention import create_attention_backend
from minisgl.core import Batch, Context, Req, set_global_ctx
from minisgl.distributed import destroy_distributed, enable_pynccl_distributed, set_tp_info
from minisgl.kvcache import create_kvcache_pool
from minisgl.layers import set_rope_device
from minisgl.models import create_model, load_weight
from minisgl.moe import create_moe_backend
from minisgl.utils import div_even, init_logger, is_sm90_supported, is_sm100_supported, torch_dtype

from .config import EngineConfig
from .graph import GraphRunner, get_free_memory, mem_GB
from .sample import BatchSamplingArgs, Sampler

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class ForwardOutput(NamedTuple):
    next_tokens_gpu: torch.Tensor
    next_tokens_cpu: torch.Tensor
    copy_done_event: torch.cuda.Event


class Engine:
    def __init__(self, config: EngineConfig):
        assert not torch.cuda.is_initialized()
        set_tp_info(rank=config.tp_info.rank, size=config.tp_info.size)
        _adjust_config(config)

        self.device = torch.device(f"cuda:{config.tp_info.rank}")
        torch.cuda.set_device(self.device)
        torch.manual_seed(42)
        self.stream = torch.cuda.Stream()
        torch.cuda.set_stream(self.stream)
        self.dtype = config.dtype
        self.ctx = Context(config.page_size)
        set_global_ctx(self.ctx)

        self.tp_cpu_group = self._init_communication(config)
        init_free_memory = self._sync_get_memory()[1]
        logger.info_rank0(f"Free memory before loading model: {mem_GB(init_free_memory)}")

        # ======================= Model initialization ========================
        set_rope_device(self.device)
        with torch.device("meta"), torch_dtype(config.dtype):
            self.model = create_model(config.model_config)
        self.model.load_state_dict(self._load_weight_state_dict(config))

        # ======================= KV cache initialization ========================
        self.num_pages = self._determine_num_pages(init_free_memory, config)
        num_tokens = self.num_pages * config.page_size
        self.ctx.kv_cache = self.kv_cache = create_kvcache_pool(
            model_config=config.model_config,
            num_pages=self.num_pages + 1,  # +1 for dummy page
            page_size=config.page_size,
            device=self.device,
            dtype=self.dtype,
        )

        # ======================= Page table initialization ========================
        # NOTE: 1. aligned to 128 bytes; 2. store raw locations instead of pages
        self.max_seq_len = min(config.max_seq_len, num_tokens)
        aligned_max_seq_len = _align_up_32(self.max_seq_len)
        self.ctx.page_table = self.page_table = torch.zeros(  # + 1 for dummy request
            (config.max_running_req + 1, aligned_max_seq_len),
            dtype=torch.int32,
            device=self.device,
        )

        # ======================= Attention & MoE backend initialization ========================
        self.ctx.attn_backend = self.attn_backend = create_attention_backend(
            config.attention_backend, config.model_config
        )
        if config.model_config.is_moe:
            self.ctx.moe_backend = self.moe_backend = create_moe_backend(config.moe_backend)

        # ======================= Sampler initialization ========================
        self.sampler = Sampler(self.device, config.model_config.vocab_size)

        post_free_memory = self._sync_get_memory()[0]
        logger.info_rank0(f"Free memory after initialization: {mem_GB(post_free_memory)}")

        # ======================= Graph capture initialization ========================
        self.dummy_req = Req(
            input_ids=torch.tensor([0], dtype=torch.int32, device="cpu"),
            table_idx=config.max_running_req,
            cached_len=0,
            output_len=1,
            uid=-1,
            sampling_params=None,  # type: ignore
            cache_handle=None,  # type: ignore
        )
        self.page_table[self.dummy_req.table_idx].fill_(num_tokens)  # point to dummy page
        self.graph_runner = GraphRunner(
            stream=self.stream,
            device=self.device,
            model=self.model,
            attn_backend=self.attn_backend,
            cuda_graph_bs=config.cuda_graph_bs,
            cuda_graph_max_bs=config.cuda_graph_max_bs,
            free_memory=init_free_memory,
            max_seq_len=aligned_max_seq_len,
            vocab_size=config.model_config.vocab_size,
            dummy_req=self.dummy_req,
        )

    def _init_communication(self, config: EngineConfig) -> torch.distributed.ProcessGroup:
        if config.tp_info.size == 1 or config.use_pynccl:
            torch.distributed.init_process_group(
                backend="gloo",
                rank=config.tp_info.rank,
                world_size=config.tp_info.size,
                timeout=timedelta(seconds=config.distributed_timeout),
                init_method=config.distributed_addr,
            )
            tp_cpu_group = torch.distributed.group.WORLD
            assert tp_cpu_group is not None
            max_bytes = (
                config.max_forward_len * config.model_config.hidden_size * self.dtype.itemsize
            )
            enable_pynccl_distributed(config.tp_info, tp_cpu_group, max_bytes)
        else:
            torch.distributed.init_process_group(
                backend="nccl",
                rank=config.tp_info.rank,
                world_size=config.tp_info.size,
                timeout=timedelta(seconds=config.distributed_timeout),
                init_method=config.distributed_addr,
            )
            tp_cpu_group = torch.distributed.new_group(backend="gloo")
            assert tp_cpu_group is not None
        return tp_cpu_group

    def _load_weight_state_dict(self, config: EngineConfig) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
        if config.use_dummy_weight:
            return {
                k: torch.randn_like(v, device=self.device)
                for k, v in self.model.state_dict().items()
            }
        else:
            return {k: v.to(self.dtype) for k, v in load_weight(config.model_path, self.device)}

    def _determine_num_pages(self, old_free_memory: int, config: EngineConfig) -> int:
        new_free_memory = self._sync_get_memory()[1]
        cache_per_page = (
            2  # key + value
            * config.model_config.head_dim
            * div_even(config.model_config.num_kv_heads, config.tp_info.size, allow_replicate=True)
            * config.page_size
            * self.dtype.itemsize
            * config.model_config.num_layers
        )
        num_pages = config.num_page_override
        if num_pages is None:
            model_memory = old_free_memory - new_free_memory
            available_memory = int(config.memory_ratio * old_free_memory) - model_memory
            num_pages = available_memory // cache_per_page

        assert num_pages > 1, "Not enough memory for KV cache, try reducing --num-pages"
        num_tokens = num_pages * config.page_size
        real_kv_size = num_pages * cache_per_page
        logger.info(f"Allocating {num_tokens} tokens for KV cache, K + V = {mem_GB(real_kv_size)}")
        return num_pages

    def _sync_get_memory(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """Get the min and max free memory across TP ranks."""
        torch.cuda.synchronize(self.device)
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats(self.device)
        free_memory = get_free_memory(self.device)
        free_mem_tensor = torch.tensor([free_memory, -free_memory], device="cpu", dtype=torch.int64)
        torch.distributed.all_reduce(
            free_mem_tensor, op=torch.distributed.ReduceOp.MIN, group=self.tp_cpu_group
        )
        min_free_memory = int(free_mem_tensor[0].item())
        max_free_memory = -int(free_mem_tensor[1].item())
        if max_free_memory - min_free_memory > 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024:
            logger.error(
                f"Memory across TP ranks are imbalanced:"
                f" min {mem_GB(min_free_memory)}, max {mem_GB(max_free_memory)}"
            )
            raise RuntimeError("Memory across TP ranks are imbalanced")

        return min_free_memory, max_free_memory

    def forward_batch(self, batch: Batch, args: BatchSamplingArgs) -> ForwardOutput:
        assert torch.cuda.current_stream() == self.stream
        with self.ctx.forward_batch(batch):
            if self.graph_runner.can_use_cuda_graph(batch):
                logits = self.graph_runner.replay(batch)
            else:
                logits = self.model.forward()

        for req in batch.reqs:
            req.complete_one()

        next_tokens_gpu = self.sampler.sample(logits[: batch.size], args).to(torch.int32)
        next_tokens_cpu = next_tokens_gpu.to("cpu", non_blocking=True)
        copy_done_event = torch.cuda.Event()
        copy_done_event.record(self.stream)
        return ForwardOutput(next_tokens_gpu, next_tokens_cpu, copy_done_event)

    def shutdown(self) -> None:
        self.graph_runner.destroy_cuda_graphs()
        torch.distributed.destroy_process_group()
        destroy_distributed()


def _align_up_32(num: int) -> int:
    return (num + 31) // 32 * 32


def _adjust_config(config: EngineConfig):
    def override(attr: str, value: Any):  # this is dangerous, use with caution
        object.__setattr__(config, attr, value)

    if config.attention_backend == "auto":
        backend = "trtllm" if is_sm100_supported() else ("fa,fi" if is_sm90_supported() else "fi")
        override("attention_backend", backend)
        logger.info_rank0(f"Auto-selected attention backend: {config.attention_backend}")

    if "trtllm" in config.attention_backend and config.page_size not in [16, 32, 64]:
        override("page_size", 64)
        logger.warning_rank0("Page size is overridden to 64 for TRTLLM backend")

    if config.model_config.is_moe and config.moe_backend == "auto":
        override("moe_backend", "fused")
        logger.info_rank0(f"Auto-selected MoE backend: {config.moe_backend}")

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List, Tuple
5	
6	import torch
7	from minisgl.core import Batch, Req
8	from minisgl.utils import init_logger
9	
10	from .utils import PendingReq
11	
12	if TYPE_CHECKING:
13	    from minisgl.kvcache import BaseCacheHandle
14	    from minisgl.message import UserMsg
15	
16	    from .cache import CacheManager
17	    from .decode import DecodeManager
18	    from .table import TableManager
19	
20	logger = init_logger(__name__)
21	
22	
23	class ChunkedReq(Req):
24	    def append_host(self, next_token: torch.Tensor) -> None:
25	        raise NotImplementedError("ChunkedReq should not be sampled")
26	
27	    @property
28	    def can_decode(self) -> bool:
29	        return False  # avoid being added to decode manager
30	
31	
32	@dataclass
33	class PrefillAdder:
34	    token_budget: int
35	    reserved_size: int
36	    cache_manager: CacheManager
37	    table_manager: TableManager
38	
39	    def _try_allocate_one(self, req: PendingReq) -> Tuple[BaseCacheHandle, int] | None:
40	        if self.table_manager.available_size == 0:
41	            return None
42	
43	        # TODO: consider host cache match case
44	        handle = self.cache_manager.match_req(req).cuda_handle
45	        cached_len = handle.cached_len
46	        # TODO: better estimate policy
47	        extend_len = req.input_len - cached_len
48	        estimated_len = extend_len + req.output_len
49	
50	        if estimated_len + self.reserved_size > self.cache_manager.available_size:
51	            return None
52	        self.cache_manager.lock(handle)
53	        if estimated_len + self.reserved_size > self.cache_manager.available_size:
54	            return self.cache_manager.unlock(handle)
55	
56	        table_idx = self.table_manager.allocate()
57	        if cached_len > 0:  # NOTE: set the cached part
58	            device_ids = self.table_manager.token_pool[table_idx][:cached_len]
59	            page_entry = self.table_manager.page_table[table_idx][:cached_len]
60	            device_ids.copy_(req.input_ids[:cached_len].pin_memory(), non_blocking=True)
61	            page_entry.copy_(handle.get_matched_indices())
62	
63	        return handle, table_idx
64	
65	    def _add_one_req(
66	        self,
67	        pending_req: PendingReq,
68	        cache_handle: BaseCacheHandle,
69	        table_idx: int,
70	        cached_len: int,
71	    ) -> Req:
72	        remain_len = pending_req.input_len - cached_len
73	        chunk_size = min(self.token_budget, remain_len)
74	        is_chunked = chunk_size < remain_len
75	        CLS = ChunkedReq if is_chunked else Req
76	        self.token_budget -= chunk_size
77	        self.reserved_size += remain_len + pending_req.output_len
78	        # NOTE: update the tokens ids only; new pages will be allocated in the scheduler
79	        _slice = slice(cached_len, cached_len + chunk_size)
80	        device_ids = self.table_manager.token_pool[table_idx, _slice]
81	        device_ids.copy_(pending_req.input_ids[_slice].pin_memory(), non_blocking=True)
82	        return CLS(
83	            input_ids=pending_req.input_ids[: cached_len + chunk_size],
84	            table_idx=table_idx,
85	            cached_len=cached_len,
86	            output_len=pending_req.output_len,
87	            uid=pending_req.uid,
88	            cache_handle=cache_handle,
89	            sampling_params=pending_req.sampling_params,
90	        )
91	
92	    def try_add_one(self, pending_req: PendingReq) -> Req | None:
93	        if self.token_budget <= 0:
94	            return None
95	
96	        if chunked_req := pending_req.chunked_req:
97	            return self._add_one_req(
98	                pending_req=pending_req,
99	                cache_handle=chunked_req.cache_handle,
100	                table_idx=chunked_req.table_idx,
101	                cached_len=chunked_req.cached_len,
102	            )
103	
104	        if resource := self._try_allocate_one(pending_req):
105	            cache_handle, table_idx = resource
106	            return self._add_one_req(
107	                pending_req=pending_req,
108	                cache_handle=cache_handle,
109	                table_idx=table_idx,
110	                cached_len=cache_handle.cached_len,
111	            )
112	
113	        return None
114	
115	
116	@dataclass
117	class PrefillManager:
118	    cache_manager: CacheManager
119	    table_manager: TableManager
120	    decode_manager: DecodeManager
121	    pending_list: List[PendingReq] = field(default_factory=list)
122	
123	    def add_one_req(self, req: UserMsg) -> None:
124	        self.pending_list.append(PendingReq(req.uid, req.input_ids, req.sampling_params))
125	
126	    def schedule_next_batch(self, prefill_budget: int) -> Batch | None:
127	        if len(self.pending_list) == 0:
128	            return None
129	
130	        # estimated offset due to in-flight decode
131	        adder = PrefillAdder(
132	            token_budget=prefill_budget,
133	            reserved_size=self.decode_manager.inflight_tokens,
134	            cache_manager=self.cache_manager,
135	            table_manager=self.table_manager,
136	        )
137	        reqs: List[Req] = []
138	        chunked_list: List[PendingReq] = []
139	        for pending_req in self.pending_list:
140	            if req := adder.try_add_one(pending_req):
141	                pending_req.chunked_req = None
142	                if isinstance(req, ChunkedReq):
143	                    pending_req.chunked_req = req
144	                    chunked_list.append(pending_req)
145	                reqs.append(req)
146	            else:
147	                break  # We cannot add more requests
148	        if len(reqs) == 0:
149	            return None
150	        self.pending_list = chunked_list + self.pending_list[len(reqs) :]
151	        return Batch(reqs=reqs, phase="prefill")
152	
153	    def abort_req(self, uid: int) -> Req | None:
154	        for i, req in enumerate(self.pending_list):
155	            if req.uid == uid:
156	                self.pending_list.pop(i)
157	                return req.chunked_req
158	        return None
159	
160	    @property
161	    def runnable(self) -> bool:
162	        return len(self.pending_list) > 0
163

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import Iterable, Set
5	
6	from minisgl.core import Batch, Req
7	
8	
9	@dataclass
10	class DecodeManager:
11	    page_size: int
12	    running_reqs: Set[Req] = field(default_factory=set)
13	
14	    def filter_reqs(self, reqs: Iterable[Req]) -> None:
15	        self.running_reqs = {req for req in self.running_reqs.union(reqs) if req.can_decode}
16	
17	    def remove_req(self, req: Req) -> None:
18	        self.running_reqs.discard(req)
19	
20	    def abort_req(self, uid: int) -> Req | None:
21	        for req in self.running_reqs:
22	            if req.uid == uid:
23	                self.running_reqs.remove(req)
24	                return req
25	        return None
26	
27	    @property
28	    def inflight_tokens(self) -> int:
29	        tokens_reserved = (self.page_size - 1) * len(self.running_reqs)  # 1 page reserved
30	        return sum(req.remain_len for req in self.running_reqs) + tokens_reserved
31	
32	    def schedule_next_batch(self) -> Batch | None:
33	        if not self.runnable:
34	            return None
35	        return Batch(reqs=sorted(self.running_reqs, key=lambda req: req.uid), phase="decode")
36	
37	    @property
38	    def runnable(self) -> bool:
39	        return len(self.running_reqs) > 0
40

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from contextlib import contextmanager
4	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List, Tuple
5	
6	import torch
7	from minisgl.core import Req
8	from minisgl.kvcache import BaseCacheHandle, MatchResult, create_prefix_cache
9	from minisgl.utils import div_ceil
10	
11	if TYPE_CHECKING:
12	    from .utils import PendingReq
13	
14	
15	class CacheManager:
16	    def __init__(self, num_pages: int, page_size: int, page_table: torch.Tensor, type: str):
17	        # The `_free_slots` follows a page-aligned manner. For example, if page_size = 2,
18	        # the `_free_slots` may look like [0, 2, 4, 6, ...], and each slot represents a page.
19	        device = page_table.device
20	        self.free_slots = torch.arange(num_pages, dtype=torch.int32, device=device) * page_size
21	        self.prefix_cache = create_prefix_cache(device=device, type=type)
22	        self.device = device
23	        self.num_pages = num_pages
24	        self.page_table = page_table
25	        self.page_size = page_size
26	
27	    def match_req(self, req: PendingReq) -> MatchResult:
28	        input_len = req.input_len
29	        assert input_len > 0, "Input length must be greater than 0."
30	        return self.prefix_cache.match_prefix(req.input_ids[: input_len - 1])
31	
32	    @property
33	    def available_size(self) -> int:
34	        return self.prefix_cache.size_info.evictable_size + len(self.free_slots) * self.page_size
35	
36	    def lock(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle) -> None:
37	        self.prefix_cache.lock_handle(handle, unlock=False)
38	
39	    def unlock(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle) -> None:
40	        self.prefix_cache.lock_handle(handle, unlock=True)
41	
42	    def allocate_paged(self, reqs: List[Req]) -> None:
43	        needed_pages = 0
44	        allocation_info: List[Tuple[int, int, int]] = []
45	        for req in reqs:
46	            first_page = div_ceil(req.cached_len, self.page_size)
47	            last_page = div_ceil(req.device_len, self.page_size)
48	            if last_page > first_page:
49	                needed_pages += last_page - first_page
50	                allocation_info.append((req.table_idx, first_page, last_page))
51	        if needed_pages > 0:
52	            allocated = self._page_to_token(self._allocate(needed_pages))
53	            _write_page_table(self.page_table, allocated, allocation_info, self.page_size)
54	
55	    def cache_req(self, req: Req, *, finished: bool) -> None:
56	        # ==================================== valid cache region ====================================
57	        # [0, req.cached_len)                       This part is valid for attention kernel read/write.
58	        # [0, old_handle.cached_len)                This part is in the prefix cache before prefill.
59	        # [old_handle.cached_len, req.cached_len)   This part is allocated by cache manager for this request.
60	        # ================================== allocated cache region ==================================
61	        # [old_handle.cached_len, cached_len)       This part was not in the prefix cache when prefill,
62	        #                                           but later cached by other requests.
63	        #                                           We must free them to avoid memory leak.
64	        # [cached_len, new_handle.cached_len)       This part is newly inserted into the prefix cache.
65	        # [new_handle.cached_len, req.cached_len)   This part is tailing part that can not inserted into the prefix cache.
66	        #                                           We should free it if the request has finished.
67	        insert_ids = req.input_ids[: req.cached_len]
68	        page_indices = self.page_table[req.table_idx, : req.cached_len]
69	        old_handle = req.cache_handle
70	        cached_len, new_handle = self.prefix_cache.insert_prefix(insert_ids, page_indices)
71	        # unlock until all operations on handle is done
72	        self.unlock(old_handle)
73	        # this part is already in the prefix cache, free it
74	        self._free(page_indices[old_handle.cached_len : cached_len])
75	        if finished:  # this tail part should be freed
76	            self._free(page_indices[new_handle.cached_len :])
77	        else:  # keep the tail part, update the handle
78	            req.cache_handle = new_handle
79	            self.lock(new_handle)
80	
81	    def check_integrity(self) -> None:
82	        self.prefix_cache.check_integrity()
83	        cache_pages = self.prefix_cache.size_info.total_size // self.page_size
84	        if len(self.free_slots) + cache_pages != self.num_pages:
85	            raise RuntimeError(
86	                "CacheManager integrity check failed:"
87	                f" free_pages({len(self.free_slots)}) +"
88	                f" cache_pages({cache_pages}) != num_pages({self.num_pages})"
89	            )
90	        if self.page_size > 1:
91	            assert torch.all(self.free_slots % self.page_size == 0)
92	
93	    @contextmanager
94	    def lazy_free_region(self):
95	        def lazy_free(indices: torch.Tensor) -> None:
96	            lazy_free_list.append(indices[:: self.page_size])
97	
98	        lazy_free_list: List[torch.Tensor] = []
99	        try:
100	            self._free = lazy_free
101	            yield
102	        finally:
103	            del self._free
104	            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots] + lazy_free_list)
105	
106	    def _allocate(self, needed_pages: int) -> torch.Tensor:
107	        if needed_pages > (free_pages := len(self.free_slots)):
108	            evicted = self.prefix_cache.evict((needed_pages - free_pages) * self.page_size)
109	            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots, evicted[:: self.page_size]])
110	            assert len(self.free_slots) >= needed_pages, "Eviction did not free enough space."
111	        allocated = self.free_slots[:needed_pages]
112	        self.free_slots = self.free_slots[needed_pages:]
113	        return allocated
114	
115	    def _free(self, indices: torch.Tensor) -> None:
116	        if len(indices) > 0:
117	            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots, indices[:: self.page_size]])
118	
119	    def _page_to_token(self, pages: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
120	        if self.page_size == 1:
121	            return pages
122	        # [X * page_size] -> [X * page_size, ..., X * page_size + page_size - 1]
123	        offsets = torch.arange(self.page_size, device=self.device, dtype=torch.int32)
124	        return (pages.unsqueeze(1) + offsets).flatten()
125	
126	
127	def _write_page_table(
128	    page_table: torch.Tensor,
129	    allocated: torch.Tensor,
130	    allocation_info: List[Tuple[int, int, int]],
131	    page_size: int,
132	) -> None:
133	    needed_tokens = len(allocated)
134	    table_idx_host = torch.empty(needed_tokens, dtype=torch.int64, pin_memory=True)
135	    positions_host = torch.empty(needed_tokens, dtype=torch.int64, pin_memory=True)
136	    offset = 0
137	    for table_idx, first_page, last_page in allocation_info:
138	        first_pos, last_pos = first_page * page_size, last_page * page_size
139	        length = last_pos - first_pos
140	        table_idx_host[offset : offset + length].fill_(table_idx)
141	        torch.arange(first_pos, last_pos, out=positions_host[offset : offset + length])
142	        offset += length
143	    assert offset == needed_tokens, "Mismatch in allocated tokens and filled tokens."
144	    table_idxs = table_idx_host.to(page_table.device, non_blocking=True)
145	    offsets = positions_host.to(page_table.device, non_blocking=True)
146	    page_table[table_idxs, offsets] = allocated
147

from __future__ import annotations

from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List, Tuple

import torch
from minisgl.core import Req
from minisgl.kvcache import BaseCacheHandle, MatchResult, create_prefix_cache
from minisgl.utils import div_ceil

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .utils import PendingReq


class CacheManager:
    def __init__(self, num_pages: int, page_size: int, page_table: torch.Tensor, type: str):
        # The `_free_slots` follows a page-aligned manner. For example, if page_size = 2,
        # the `_free_slots` may look like [0, 2, 4, 6, ...], and each slot represents a page.
        device = page_table.device
        self.free_slots = torch.arange(num_pages, dtype=torch.int32, device=device) * page_size
        self.prefix_cache = create_prefix_cache(device=device, type=type)
        self.device = device
        self.num_pages = num_pages
        self.page_table = page_table
        self.page_size = page_size

    def match_req(self, req: PendingReq) -> MatchResult:
        input_len = req.input_len
        assert input_len > 0, "Input length must be greater than 0."
        return self.prefix_cache.match_prefix(req.input_ids[: input_len - 1])

    @property
    def available_size(self) -> int:
        return self.prefix_cache.size_info.evictable_size + len(self.free_slots) * self.page_size

    def lock(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle) -> None:
        self.prefix_cache.lock_handle(handle, unlock=False)

    def unlock(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle) -> None:
        self.prefix_cache.lock_handle(handle, unlock=True)

    def allocate_paged(self, reqs: List[Req]) -> None:
        needed_pages = 0
        allocation_info: List[Tuple[int, int, int]] = []
        for req in reqs:
            first_page = div_ceil(req.cached_len, self.page_size)
            last_page = div_ceil(req.device_len, self.page_size)
            if last_page > first_page:
                needed_pages += last_page - first_page
                allocation_info.append((req.table_idx, first_page, last_page))
        if needed_pages > 0:
            allocated = self._page_to_token(self._allocate(needed_pages))
            _write_page_table(self.page_table, allocated, allocation_info, self.page_size)

    def cache_req(self, req: Req, *, finished: bool) -> None:
        # ==================================== valid cache region ====================================
        # [0, req.cached_len)                       This part is valid for attention kernel read/write.
        # [0, old_handle.cached_len)                This part is in the prefix cache before prefill.
        # [old_handle.cached_len, req.cached_len)   This part is allocated by cache manager for this request.
        # ================================== allocated cache region ==================================
        # [old_handle.cached_len, cached_len)       This part was not in the prefix cache when prefill,
        #                                           but later cached by other requests.
        #                                           We must free them to avoid memory leak.
        # [cached_len, new_handle.cached_len)       This part is newly inserted into the prefix cache.
        # [new_handle.cached_len, req.cached_len)   This part is tailing part that can not inserted into the prefix cache.
        #                                           We should free it if the request has finished.
        insert_ids = req.input_ids[: req.cached_len]
        page_indices = self.page_table[req.table_idx, : req.cached_len]
        old_handle = req.cache_handle
        cached_len, new_handle = self.prefix_cache.insert_prefix(insert_ids, page_indices)
        # unlock until all operations on handle is done
        self.unlock(old_handle)
        # this part is already in the prefix cache, free it
        self._free(page_indices[old_handle.cached_len : cached_len])
        if finished:  # this tail part should be freed
            self._free(page_indices[new_handle.cached_len :])
        else:  # keep the tail part, update the handle
            req.cache_handle = new_handle
            self.lock(new_handle)

    def check_integrity(self) -> None:
        self.prefix_cache.check_integrity()
        cache_pages = self.prefix_cache.size_info.total_size // self.page_size
        if len(self.free_slots) + cache_pages != self.num_pages:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "CacheManager integrity check failed:"
                f" free_pages({len(self.free_slots)}) +"
                f" cache_pages({cache_pages}) != num_pages({self.num_pages})"
            )
        if self.page_size > 1:
            assert torch.all(self.free_slots % self.page_size == 0)

    @contextmanager
    def lazy_free_region(self):
        def lazy_free(indices: torch.Tensor) -> None:
            lazy_free_list.append(indices[:: self.page_size])

        lazy_free_list: List[torch.Tensor] = []
        try:
            self._free = lazy_free
            yield
        finally:
            del self._free
            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots] + lazy_free_list)

    def _allocate(self, needed_pages: int) -> torch.Tensor:
        if needed_pages > (free_pages := len(self.free_slots)):
            evicted = self.prefix_cache.evict((needed_pages - free_pages) * self.page_size)
            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots, evicted[:: self.page_size]])
            assert len(self.free_slots) >= needed_pages, "Eviction did not free enough space."
        allocated = self.free_slots[:needed_pages]
        self.free_slots = self.free_slots[needed_pages:]
        return allocated

    def _free(self, indices: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        if len(indices) > 0:
            self.free_slots = torch.cat([self.free_slots, indices[:: self.page_size]])

    def _page_to_token(self, pages: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if self.page_size == 1:
            return pages
        # [X * page_size] -> [X * page_size, ..., X * page_size + page_size - 1]
        offsets = torch.arange(self.page_size, device=self.device, dtype=torch.int32)
        return (pages.unsqueeze(1) + offsets).flatten()


def _write_page_table(
    page_table: torch.Tensor,
    allocated: torch.Tensor,
    allocation_info: List[Tuple[int, int, int]],
    page_size: int,
) -> None:
    needed_tokens = len(allocated)
    table_idx_host = torch.empty(needed_tokens, dtype=torch.int64, pin_memory=True)
    positions_host = torch.empty(needed_tokens, dtype=torch.int64, pin_memory=True)
    offset = 0
    for table_idx, first_page, last_page in allocation_info:
        first_pos, last_pos = first_page * page_size, last_page * page_size
        length = last_pos - first_pos
        table_idx_host[offset : offset + length].fill_(table_idx)
        torch.arange(first_pos, last_pos, out=positions_host[offset : offset + length])
        offset += length
    assert offset == needed_tokens, "Mismatch in allocated tokens and filled tokens."
    table_idxs = table_idx_host.to(page_table.device, non_blocking=True)
    offsets = positions_host.to(page_table.device, non_blocking=True)
    page_table[table_idxs, offsets] = allocated

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Final, List

import torch
from minisgl.message import BaseBackendMsg, BaseTokenizerMsg, BatchTokenizerMsg, DetokenizeMsg
from minisgl.utils import ZmqPubQueue, ZmqPullQueue, ZmqPushQueue, ZmqSubQueue, init_logger

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .config import SchedulerConfig

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class SchedulerIOMixin:
    """
    Mixin class for Scheduler I/O operations.

    This class handles the communication between the scheduler and the tokenizer.

    Public Utilities:
        receive_msg: Function to receive messages from the tokenizer.
        send_result: Function to send results back to the tokenizer.
        sync_all_ranks: Function to synchronize all ranks on CPU side.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: SchedulerConfig, tp_cpu_group: torch.distributed.ProcessGroup):
        tp_info = config.tp_info
        self.tp_cpu_group: Final = tp_cpu_group
        if config.offline_mode:
            self.receive_msg = self.offline_receive_msg
            self.send_result = self.offline_send_result
            return  # early exit

        if tp_info.is_primary():
            self._recv_from_tokenizer: Final = ZmqPullQueue(
                config.zmq_backend_addr,
                create=True,
                decoder=BaseBackendMsg.decoder,
            )
            self._send_into_tokenizer: Final = ZmqPushQueue(
                config.zmq_detokenizer_addr,
                create=config.backend_create_detokenizer_link,
                encoder=BaseTokenizerMsg.encoder,
            )

        recv = self._recv_msg_single_rank
        send = self._reply_tokenizer_rank0
        if tp_info.size > 1:
            if tp_info.is_primary():
                recv = self._recv_msg_multi_rank0
                self._send_into_ranks: Final = ZmqPubQueue(
                    config.zmq_scheduler_broadcast_addr, create=True, encoder=BaseBackendMsg.encoder
                )
            else:
                recv = self._recv_msg_multi_rank1
                send = self._reply_tokenizer_rank1
                self._recv_from_rank0: Final = ZmqSubQueue(
                    config.zmq_scheduler_broadcast_addr,
                    create=False,
                    decoder=BaseBackendMsg.decoder,
                )

        self.receive_msg = recv
        self.send_result = send

    def run_when_idle(self):
        raise NotImplementedError("should be implemented")

    def offline_receive_msg(self, blocking: bool = False) -> List[BaseBackendMsg]:
        raise NotImplementedError("should be implemented")

    def offline_send_result(self, reply: List[DetokenizeMsg]) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError("should be implemented")

    def sync_all_ranks(self) -> None:
        self.tp_cpu_group.barrier().wait()

    def _recv_msg_single_rank(self, blocking: bool = False) -> List[BaseBackendMsg]:
        pending_msgs: List[BaseBackendMsg] = []
        if blocking:
            self.run_when_idle()
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_tokenizer.get())
        while not self._recv_from_tokenizer.empty():
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_tokenizer.get())
        return pending_msgs

    def _recv_msg_multi_rank0(self, blocking: bool = False) -> List[BaseBackendMsg]:
        pending_msgs: List[BaseBackendMsg] = []
        if blocking:
            self.run_when_idle()
            raw = self._recv_from_tokenizer.get_raw()
            self._send_into_ranks.put_raw(raw)
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_tokenizer.decode(raw))

        pending_raw_msgs: List[bytes] = []
        while not self._recv_from_tokenizer.empty():
            pending_raw_msgs.append(self._recv_from_tokenizer.get_raw())

        # broadcast the number of raw messages to all ranks
        src_tensor = torch.tensor(len(pending_raw_msgs))
        self.tp_cpu_group.broadcast(src_tensor, root=0).wait()

        for raw in pending_raw_msgs:
            self._send_into_ranks.put_raw(raw)
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_tokenizer.decode(raw))
        return pending_msgs

    def _recv_msg_multi_rank1(self, blocking: bool = False) -> List[BaseBackendMsg]:
        pending_msgs: List[BaseBackendMsg] = []
        if blocking:
            self.run_when_idle()
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_rank0.get())

        # ensure all ranks have the same number of raw messages
        dst_tensor = torch.tensor(-1)
        self.tp_cpu_group.broadcast(dst_tensor, root=0).wait()
        dst_length = int(dst_tensor.item())

        for _ in range(dst_length):
            pending_msgs.append(self._recv_from_rank0.get())
        return pending_msgs

    def _reply_tokenizer_rank0(self, reply: List[DetokenizeMsg]) -> None:
        num_reply = len(reply)
        logger.debug_rank0(f"Replying to tokenizer: {num_reply} messages")
        if num_reply == 1:
            self._send_into_tokenizer.put(reply[0])
        elif num_reply > 1:
            self._send_into_tokenizer.put(BatchTokenizerMsg(data=reply))  # type: ignore

    def _reply_tokenizer_rank1(self, reply: List[DetokenizeMsg]) -> None:
        _ = reply  # do nothing for non-primary ranks

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from typing import NamedTuple
6	
7	import torch
8	
9	
10	class BaseKVCachePool(ABC):
11	    """
12	    Base class for key-value caches.
13	    This class defines the interface for key-value caches used.
14	    """
15	
16	    @abstractmethod
17	    def k_cache(self, index: int) -> torch.Tensor: ...
18	
19	    @abstractmethod
20	    def v_cache(self, index: int) -> torch.Tensor: ...
21	
22	    @abstractmethod
23	    def store_kv(
24	        self, k: torch.Tensor, v: torch.Tensor, out_loc: torch.Tensor, layer_id: int
25	    ) -> None: ...
26	
27	    @property
28	    @abstractmethod
29	    def device(self) -> torch.device: ...
30	
31	    @property
32	    @abstractmethod
33	    def dtype(self) -> torch.dtype: ...
34	
35	    @property
36	    @abstractmethod
37	    def num_layers(self) -> int: ...
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True)
41	class BaseCacheHandle(ABC):
42	    cached_len: int
43	
44	    @abstractmethod
45	    def get_matched_indices(self) -> torch.Tensor: ...
46	
47	
48	class SizeInfo(NamedTuple):
49	    evictable_size: int
50	    protected_size: int
51	
52	    @property
53	    def total_size(self) -> int:
54	        return self.evictable_size + self.protected_size
55	
56	
57	class InsertResult(NamedTuple):
58	    cached_len: int  # length already in cache before insertion (should be freed)
59	    handle: BaseCacheHandle  # cache handle for the inserted prefix
60	
61	
62	class MatchResult(NamedTuple):
63	    cuda_handle: BaseCacheHandle
64	    # TODO: support HiCache
65	
66	
67	class BasePrefixCache(ABC):
68	    @abstractmethod
69	    def lock_handle(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle, unlock: bool = False) -> None:
70	        """
71	        Lock or unlock a cache handle.
72	        This operation will not modify the cache, but change the size info only.
73	        When a handle is locked, it cannot be evicted.
74	        Handles must be locked before the previously-returned tensor of `match_prefix` is used.
75	        Otherwise it may be evicted by calling evict.
76	
77	        Args:
78	            handle (BaseCacheHandle): The cache handle to lock or unlock.
79	            unlock (bool): Whether to unlock the handle. Defaults to False.
80	        """
81	
82	    @abstractmethod
83	    def match_prefix(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor) -> MatchResult:
84	        """
85	        Match prefix and return the indices of the matched prefix in the cache.
86	        This operation will not modify the cache.
87	        The returned indices is only safe to use when the handle is locked.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            input_ids (torch.Tensor): The input ids to match. Shape: (seq_len,)
91	        Returns:
92	            MatchResult: The match result containing the cache handles.
93	        """
94	
95	    @abstractmethod
96	    def insert_prefix(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> InsertResult:
97	        """
98	        Insert a new prefix into the cache.
99	        This operation will modify the cache.
100	        Args:
101	            input_ids (torch.Tensor): The input ids to insert. Shape: (seq_len,)
102	            indices (torch.Tensor): The indices to store the new prefix. Shape: (seq_len,)
103	
104	        Returns:
105	            InsertResult: The result of the insertion.
106	        """
107	
108	    @abstractmethod
109	    def evict(self, size: int) -> torch.Tensor:
110	        """
111	        Evict some prefixes from the cache to free up space.
112	        This operation will modify the cache.
113	        Note that evict 0 is always safe and does nothing.
114	        Note that the actual evict size may be larger than the requested size.
115	        Args:
116	            size (int): The size to evict.
117	
118	        Returns:
119	            torch.Tensor: The indices evicted. Shape: (evict_size,)
120	        Raises:
121	            RuntimeError: If the requested size is larger than the evictable size.
122	        """
123	
124	    @abstractmethod
125	    def reset(self) -> None:
126	        """Reset the cache manager and the underlying cache."""
127	
128	    @property
129	    @abstractmethod
130	    def size_info(self) -> SizeInfo:
131	        """Get the size information of the cache."""
132	
133	    @abstractmethod
134	    def check_integrity(self) -> None:
135	        """Check the integrity of the cache. Raise an error if the cache is corrupted."""
136

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import heapq
4	import time
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Tuple, TypeAlias
7	
8	import torch
9	from minisgl.core import get_global_ctx
10	from minisgl.utils import align_down
11	
12	from .base import BaseCacheHandle, BasePrefixCache, InsertResult, MatchResult, SizeInfo
13	
14	KEY_FN: TypeAlias = Callable[[torch.Tensor], Any]
15	
16	
17	class RadixTreeNode:
18	    counter: int = 0
19	
20	    def __init__(self, key_fn: KEY_FN, tic: int | None = None) -> None:
21	        self.key_fn = key_fn
22	        self.children: Dict[Any, RadixTreeNode] = {}
23	        self._parent: RadixTreeNode | None = None
24	        self.ref_count: int = 0
25	        self.uuid = RadixTreeNode.counter
26	        RadixTreeNode.counter += 1
27	        self.timestamp = tic or time.monotonic_ns()
28	
29	        # these fields should be updated later
30	        self._key: torch.Tensor
31	        self._value: torch.Tensor
32	        self._length: int
33	
34	    def set_key_value(self, key: torch.Tensor, value: torch.Tensor) -> None:
35	        assert len(key) == len(value)
36	        self._key = key
37	        self._value = value
38	        self._length = len(key)
39	
40	    def set_parent(self, parent: RadixTreeNode) -> None:
41	        self._parent = parent
42	        parent.children[self.key_fn(self._key)] = self
43	
44	    @property
45	    def length(self) -> int:
46	        return self._length
47	
48	    @property
49	    def parent(self) -> RadixTreeNode:
50	        assert self._parent is not None
51	        return self._parent
52	
53	    @property
54	    def value(self) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        return self._value
56	
57	    def is_root(self) -> bool:
58	        return self._parent is None
59	
60	    def is_leaf(self) -> bool:
61	        return len(self.children) == 0
62	
63	    def get_match_len(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor) -> int:
64	        from minisgl.kernel import fast_compare_key
65	
66	        # compare key and input_ids, find the first diff
67	        return fast_compare_key(self._key, input_ids)
68	
69	    def split_at(self, pos: int) -> RadixTreeNode:
70	        assert 0 < pos < self.length
71	        parent = self.parent
72	
73	        new_node = RadixTreeNode(self.key_fn, self.timestamp)
74	        new_node.set_key_value(self._key[:pos], self._value[:pos])
75	        new_node.set_parent(parent)
76	        new_node.ref_count = self.ref_count
77	
78	        self.set_key_value(self._key[pos:], self._value[pos:])
79	        self.set_parent(new_node)
80	
81	        return new_node
82	
83	    def __lt__(self, other: RadixTreeNode) -> bool:
84	        return self.timestamp < other.timestamp
85	
86	
87	@dataclass(frozen=True)
88	class RadixCacheHandle(BaseCacheHandle):
89	    node: RadixTreeNode
90	
91	    def get_matched_indices(self) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        node = self.node
93	        value_list: List[torch.Tensor] = []
94	        while not node.is_root():
95	            value_list.append(node.value)
96	            node = node.parent
97	        value_list.reverse()
98	        return torch.cat(value_list)
99	
100	
101	class RadixPrefixCache(BasePrefixCache):
102	    def __init__(self, device: torch.device):
103	        super().__init__()
104	        self.device = device
105	        self.page_size = get_global_ctx().page_size
106	        self.key_fn = _get_key_fn(self.page_size)
107	        self.empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
108	        self.evictable_size = 0
109	        self.protected_size = 0
110	        self.root_node = RadixTreeNode(self.key_fn)
111	        self.root_node.ref_count = 1  # root is always protected
112	
113	    def lock_handle(self, handle: BaseCacheHandle, unlock: bool = False) -> None:
114	        assert isinstance(handle, RadixCacheHandle)
115	        node = handle.node
116	        if unlock:
117	            while not node.is_root():
118	                node.ref_count -= 1
119	                assert node.ref_count >= 0
120	                if node.ref_count == 0:
121	                    self.evictable_size += node.length
122	                    self.protected_size -= node.length
123	                node = node.parent
124	        else:
125	            while not node.is_root():
126	                if node.ref_count == 0:
127	                    self.evictable_size -= node.length
128	                    self.protected_size += node.length
129	                node.ref_count += 1
130	                node = node.parent
131	
132	    def match_prefix(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor) -> MatchResult:
133	        node, prefix_len = self._tree_walk(input_ids)
134	        return MatchResult(RadixCacheHandle(prefix_len, node))
135	
136	    def insert_prefix(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> InsertResult:
137	        insert_len = align_down(len(input_ids), self.page_size)
138	        input_ids, indices = input_ids[:insert_len], indices[:insert_len]
139	        node, prefix_len = self._tree_walk(input_ids)
140	        if prefix_len != insert_len:  # NOTE: prefix_len < insert_len
141	            new_node = RadixTreeNode(self.key_fn)
142	            new_node.set_key_value(input_ids[prefix_len:], indices[prefix_len:].clone())
143	            new_node.set_parent(node)
144	            self.evictable_size += new_node.length
145	            node = new_node
146	        return InsertResult(prefix_len, RadixCacheHandle(insert_len, node))
147	
148	    def evict(self, size: int) -> torch.Tensor:
149	        if size == 0:
150	            return self.empty_tensor
151	        assert (
152	            size <= self.evictable_size
153	        ), f"Cannot evict {size}, only {self.evictable_size} is evictable"
154	
155	        leave_nodes = self._collect_leave_nodes_for_evict()
156	        heapq.heapify(leave_nodes)
157	        evicted_indices: List[torch.Tensor] = []
158	        evicted_size = 0
159	
160	        while evicted_size < size:
161	            assert (
162	                leave_nodes
163	            ), f"Cannot evict enough cache, need {size}, only {evicted_size} evicted"
164	            node = heapq.heappop(leave_nodes)
165	            assert node.ref_count == 0 and node.is_leaf() and not node.is_root()
166	            evicted_size += node.length
167	            evicted_indices.append(node.value)
168	            self.evictable_size -= node.length
169	            parent = node.parent
170	            del parent.children[self.key_fn(node._key)]
171	            # NOTE: root is always protected, so won't be evicted
172	            if parent.is_leaf() and parent.ref_count == 0:
173	                heapq.heappush(leave_nodes, parent)
174	
175	        return torch.cat(evicted_indices)
176	
177	    def reset(self) -> None:
178	        raise NotImplementedError("RadixManager.reset is not implemented")
179	
180	    @property
181	    def size_info(self) -> SizeInfo:
182	        return SizeInfo(
183	            evictable_size=self.evictable_size,
184	            protected_size=self.protected_size,
185	        )
186	
187	    def check_integrity(self) -> None:
188	        pass
189	
190	    def _collect_leave_nodes_for_evict(self) -> List[RadixTreeNode]:
191	        nodes: List[RadixTreeNode] = [self.root_node]
192	        leave_nodes: List[RadixTreeNode] = []
193	
194	        while len(nodes) > 0:
195	            node = nodes.pop()
196	            if node.is_leaf():
197	                if node.ref_count == 0:
198	                    leave_nodes.append(node)
199	            else:
200	                for child in node.children.values():
201	                    nodes.append(child)
202	
203	        return leave_nodes
204	
205	    def _tree_walk(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[RadixTreeNode, int]:
206	        prefix_len = 0
207	        indice_len = len(input_ids)
208	        node = self.root_node
209	        tic = time.monotonic_ns()
210	
211	        while prefix_len < indice_len:
212	            child_node = node.children.get(self.key_fn(input_ids[prefix_len:]))
213	            if child_node is None:
214	                return node, prefix_len
215	            node = child_node  # walk to child node
216	
217	            # NOTE: at least 1 page is matched, so match_len >= page_size
218	            match_len = node.get_match_len(input_ids[prefix_len:])
219	            match_len = align_down(match_len, self.page_size)
220	            prefix_len += match_len
221	
222	            # need to split the node if not fully matched
223	            if match_len != node.length:
224	                node = node.split_at(match_len)
225	                node.timestamp = tic
226	                return node, prefix_len
227	
228	            # update timestamp for accessed node
229	            node.timestamp = tic
230	
231	        return node, prefix_len
232	
233	
234	def _get_key_fn(page_size: int) -> KEY_FN:
235	    if page_size == 1:
236	        return lambda x: x[0].item()
237	    return lambda x: tuple(x[:page_size].tolist())
238

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Any, Dict, Type
4	
5	import numpy as np
6	import torch
7	
8	
9	def _serialize_any(value: Any) -> Any:
10	    if isinstance(value, dict):
11	        return {k: _serialize_any(v) for k, v in value.items()}
12	    elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
13	        return type(value)(_serialize_any(v) for v in value)
14	    elif isinstance(value, (int, float, str, type(None), bool, bytes)):
15	        return value
16	    else:
17	        return serialize_type(value)
18	
19	
20	def serialize_type(self) -> Dict:
21	    # find all member variables
22	    serialized = {}
23	
24	    if isinstance(self, torch.Tensor):
25	        assert self.dim() == 1, "we can only serialize 1D tensor for now"
26	        serialized["__type__"] = "Tensor"
27	        serialized["buffer"] = self.numpy().tobytes()
28	        serialized["dtype"] = str(self.dtype)
29	        return serialized
30	
31	    # normal type
32	    serialized["__type__"] = self.__class__.__name__
33	    for k, v in self.__dict__.items():
34	        serialized[k] = _serialize_any(v)
35	    return serialized
36	
37	
38	def _deserialize_any(cls_map: Dict[str, Type], data: Any) -> Any:
39	    if isinstance(data, dict):
40	        if "__type__" in data:
41	            return deserialize_type(cls_map, data)
42	        else:
43	            return {k: _deserialize_any(cls_map, v) for k, v in data.items()}
44	    elif isinstance(data, (list, tuple)):
45	        return type(data)(_deserialize_any(cls_map, d) for d in data)
46	    elif isinstance(data, (int, float, str, type(None), bool, bytes)):
47	        return data
48	    else:
49	        raise ValueError(f"Cannot deserialize type {type(data)}")
50	
51	
52	def deserialize_type(cls_map: Dict[str, Type], data: Dict) -> Any:
53	    type_name = data["__type__"]
54	    # we can only serialize 1D tensor for now
55	    if type_name == "Tensor":
56	        buffer = data["buffer"]
57	        dtype_str = data["dtype"].replace("torch.", "")
58	        np_dtype = getattr(np, dtype_str)
59	        assert isinstance(buffer, bytes)
60	        np_tensor = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np_dtype)
61	        return torch.from_numpy(np_tensor.copy())
62	
63	    cls = cls_map[type_name]
64	    kwargs = {}
65	    for k, v in data.items():
66	        if k == "__type__":
67	            continue
68	        kwargs[k] = _deserialize_any(cls_map, v)
69	    return cls(**kwargs)
70

import importlib

from .config import ModelConfig

_MODEL_REGISTRY = {
    "LlamaForCausalLM": (".llama", "LlamaForCausalLM"),
    "Qwen2ForCausalLM": (".qwen2", "Qwen2ForCausalLM"),
    "Qwen3ForCausalLM": (".qwen3", "Qwen3ForCausalLM"),
    "Qwen3MoeForCausalLM": (".qwen3_moe", "Qwen3MoeForCausalLM"),
    "MistralForCausalLM": (".mistral", "MistralForCausalLM"),
    "Mistral3ForConditionalGeneration": (".mistral", "MistralForCausalLM"),
}


def get_model_class(model_architecture: str, model_config: ModelConfig):
    if model_architecture not in _MODEL_REGISTRY:
        raise ValueError(f"Model architecture {model_architecture} not supported")
    module_path, class_name = _MODEL_REGISTRY[model_architecture]
    module = importlib.import_module(module_path, package=__package__)
    model_cls = getattr(module, class_name)
    return model_cls(model_config)


__all__ = ["get_model_class"]

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Tuple

import torch
from minisgl.core import get_global_ctx
from minisgl.layers import BaseOP, OPList, ParallelLMHead, RMSNormFused, VocabParallelEmbedding
from minisgl.utils import nvtx_annotate

from .base import BaseLLMModel
from .utils import GatedMLP as LlamaMLP
from .utils import RopeAttn as LlamaAttn

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .config import ModelConfig


class LlamaDecoderLayer(BaseOP):
    def __init__(self, config: ModelConfig, layer_id: int):
        self.self_attn = LlamaAttn(config, layer_id)
        self.mlp = LlamaMLP(config)
        self.input_layernorm = RMSNormFused(
            size=config.hidden_size,
            eps=config.rms_norm_eps,
        )
        self.post_attention_layernorm = RMSNormFused(
            size=config.hidden_size,
            eps=config.rms_norm_eps,
        )

        self._layer_id = layer_id

    @nvtx_annotate("Layer_{}", layer_id_field="_layer_id")
    def forward(
        self,
        x: torch.Tensor,
        residual: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
        x, residual = self.input_layernorm.forward(x, residual)
        x = self.self_attn.forward(x)
        x, residual = self.post_attention_layernorm.forward(x, residual)
        x = self.mlp.forward(x)
        return x, residual


class LlamaModel(BaseOP):
    def __init__(self, config: ModelConfig):
        self.embed_tokens = VocabParallelEmbedding(
            num_embeddings=config.vocab_size,
            embedding_dim=config.hidden_size,
        )
        self.layers = OPList(
            [LlamaDecoderLayer(config, layer_id) for layer_id in range(config.num_layers)]
        )
        self.norm = RMSNormFused(
            size=config.hidden_size,
            eps=config.rms_norm_eps,
        )

    def forward(self, input_ids: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.embed_tokens.forward(input_ids)
        residual: torch.Tensor | None = None
        for layer in self.layers.op_list:
            x, residual = layer.forward(x, residual)
        return self.norm.forward(x, residual)[0]


class LlamaForCausalLM(BaseLLMModel):
    def __init__(self, config: ModelConfig):
        self.model = LlamaModel(config)
        self.lm_head = ParallelLMHead(
            num_embeddings=config.vocab_size,
            embedding_dim=config.hidden_size,
            tie_word_embeddings=config.tie_word_embeddings,
            tied_embedding=self.model.embed_tokens if config.tie_word_embeddings else None,
        )
        super().__init__()

    def forward(self) -> torch.Tensor:
        output = self.model.forward(get_global_ctx().batch.input_ids)
        logits = self.lm_head.forward(output)
        return logits


__all__ = ["LlamaForCausalLM"]

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from abc import abstractmethod
4	from typing import Any, Dict, Generic, List, TypeAlias, TypeVar
5	
6	import torch
7	
8	_STATE_DICT: TypeAlias = Dict[str, torch.Tensor]
9	
10	
11	def _concat_prefix(prefix: str, name: str) -> str:
12	    return f"{prefix}.{name}" if prefix else name
13	
14	
15	class BaseOP:
16	    @abstractmethod
17	    def forward(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any: ...
18	
19	    def state_dict(self, *, prefix: str = "", result: _STATE_DICT | None = None) -> _STATE_DICT:
20	        result = result if result is not None else {}
21	
22	        for name, param in self.__dict__.items():
23	            if name.startswith("_"):
24	                continue
25	            if isinstance(param, torch.Tensor):
26	                result[_concat_prefix(prefix, name)] = param
27	            elif isinstance(param, BaseOP):
28	                param.state_dict(prefix=_concat_prefix(prefix, name), result=result)
29	
30	        return result
31	
32	    def load_state_dict(
33	        self,
34	        state_dict: _STATE_DICT,
35	        *,
36	        prefix: str = "",
37	        _internal: bool = False,
38	    ) -> None:
39	        for name, param in self.__dict__.items():
40	            if name.startswith("_"):
41	                continue
42	            if isinstance(param, torch.Tensor):
43	                item = state_dict.pop(_concat_prefix(prefix, name))
44	                assert isinstance(item, torch.Tensor)
45	                assert param.shape == item.shape and param.dtype == item.dtype
46	                setattr(self, name, item)
47	            elif isinstance(param, BaseOP):
48	                param.load_state_dict(
49	                    state_dict, prefix=_concat_prefix(prefix, name), _internal=True
50	                )
51	
52	        if not _internal and state_dict:
53	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected keys in state_dict: {list(state_dict.keys())}")
54	
55	
56	class StateLessOP(BaseOP):
57	    def __init__(self):
58	        super().__init__()
59	
60	    def load_state_dict(
61	        self,
62	        state_dict: _STATE_DICT,
63	        *,
64	        prefix: str = "",
65	        _internal: bool = False,
66	    ) -> None:
67	        if not _internal and state_dict:
68	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected keys in state_dict: {list(state_dict.keys())}")
69	
70	    def state_dict(self, *, prefix: str = "", result: _STATE_DICT | None = None) -> _STATE_DICT:
71	        return result if result is not None else {}
72	
73	
74	T = TypeVar("T", bound=BaseOP)
75	
76	
77	class OPList(BaseOP, Generic[T]):
78	    def __init__(self, ops: List[T]):
79	        super().__init__()
80	        self.op_list = ops
81	
82	    def state_dict(self, *, prefix: str = "", result: _STATE_DICT | None = None) -> _STATE_DICT:
83	        result = result if result is not None else {}
84	        for i, op in enumerate(self.op_list):
85	            op.state_dict(prefix=_concat_prefix(prefix, str(i)), result=result)
86	        return result
87	
88	    def load_state_dict(
89	        self,
90	        state_dict: _STATE_DICT,
91	        *,
92	        prefix: str = "",
93	        _internal: bool = False,
94	    ) -> None:
95	        for i, op in enumerate(self.op_list):
96	            op.load_state_dict(state_dict, prefix=_concat_prefix(prefix, str(i)), _internal=True)
97	
98	        if not _internal and state_dict:
99	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected keys in state_dict: {list(state_dict.keys())}")

from __future__ import annotations

import gc
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Dict, List

import torch
from minisgl.core import Batch, Req, get_global_ctx
from minisgl.distributed import get_tp_info
from minisgl.utils import init_logger
from tqdm import tqdm

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from minisgl.attention import BaseAttnBackend
    from minisgl.models import BaseLLMModel

logger = init_logger(__name__)


@dataclass
class GraphCaptureBuffer:
    input_ids: torch.Tensor
    out_loc: torch.Tensor
    positions: torch.Tensor
    logits: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def init(cls, bs: int, vocab_size: int, device: torch.device) -> GraphCaptureBuffer:
        return GraphCaptureBuffer(
            input_ids=torch.zeros(bs, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
            out_loc=torch.zeros(bs, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
            positions=torch.zeros(bs, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
            logits=torch.empty(bs, vocab_size, dtype=torch.float32, device=device),
        )

    def set_batch(self, batch: Batch) -> None:
        _slice = slice(batch.padded_size)
        batch.input_ids = self.input_ids[_slice]
        batch.out_loc = self.out_loc[_slice]
        batch.positions = self.positions[_slice]

    def copy_from(self, batch: Batch) -> None:
        _slice = slice(batch.padded_size)
        self.input_ids[_slice] = batch.input_ids
        self.out_loc[_slice] = batch.out_loc
        self.positions[_slice] = batch.positions


def _determine_cuda_graph_bs(
    cuda_graph_bs: List[int] | None,
    cuda_graph_max_bs: int | None,
    free_memory: int,
) -> List[int]:
    if cuda_graph_bs is not None:
        return cuda_graph_bs

    free_memory_gb = free_memory / (1 << 30)
    if cuda_graph_max_bs is None:
        if free_memory_gb > 80:  # H200
            cuda_graph_max_bs = 256
        else:
            cuda_graph_max_bs = 160

    if cuda_graph_max_bs < 1:
        return []

    return [1, 2, 4] + list(range(8, cuda_graph_max_bs + 1, 8))


def mem_GB(size: int) -> str:
    return f"{size / (1024**3):.2f} GiB"


def get_free_memory(device: torch.device) -> int:
    return torch.cuda.mem_get_info(device)[0]


class GraphRunner:
    def __init__(
        self,
        stream: torch.cuda.Stream,
        device: torch.device,
        model: BaseLLMModel,
        attn_backend: BaseAttnBackend,
        cuda_graph_bs: List[int] | None,
        cuda_graph_max_bs: int | None,
        free_memory: int,
        max_seq_len: int,
        vocab_size: int,
        dummy_req: Req,
    ) -> None:
        cuda_graph_bs = _determine_cuda_graph_bs(
            cuda_graph_bs=cuda_graph_bs,
            cuda_graph_max_bs=cuda_graph_max_bs,
            free_memory=free_memory,
        )
        self.attn_backend = attn_backend
        self.max_graph_bs = max(cuda_graph_bs) if cuda_graph_bs else 0
        self.graph_bs_list = sorted(cuda_graph_bs)
        self.dummy_req = dummy_req
        self.stream = stream
        self.device = device
        self._capture_graphs(max_seq_len, vocab_size, model)

    def _capture_graphs(self, max_seq_len: int, vocab_size: int, model: BaseLLMModel):
        self.graph_map: Dict[int, torch.cuda.CUDAGraph] = {}
        if self.max_graph_bs == 0:
            return logger.info_rank0("CUDA graph is disabled.")

        self.attn_backend.init_capture_graph(max_seq_len=max_seq_len, bs_list=self.graph_bs_list)

        torch.cuda.synchronize(self.device)
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats(self.device)

        logger.info_rank0(f"Start capturing CUDA graphs with sizes: {self.graph_bs_list}")
        free_memory = get_free_memory(self.device)
        logger.info_rank0(f"Free GPU memory before capturing CUDA graphs: {mem_GB(free_memory)}")

        self.buffer = GraphCaptureBuffer.init(self.max_graph_bs, vocab_size, self.device)

        pbar = tqdm(
            sorted(self.graph_bs_list, reverse=True),
            desc="Preparing for capturing CUDA graphs...",
            unit="batch",
            disable=not get_tp_info().is_primary(),  # disable for non-primary ranks
        )
        pool = None
        for bs in pbar:
            free_memory = get_free_memory(self.device)
            pbar.desc = f"Capturing graphs: bs = {bs:<3} | avail_mem = {mem_GB(free_memory)}"
            pbar.refresh()
            graph = torch.cuda.CUDAGraph()
            batch = Batch(reqs=[self.dummy_req] * bs, phase="decode")
            batch.padded_reqs = batch.reqs
            self.attn_backend.prepare_for_capture(batch)
            self.buffer.set_batch(batch)
            with get_global_ctx().forward_batch(batch):
                self.buffer.logits[:bs] = model.forward()
                with torch.cuda.graph(graph, pool=pool, stream=self.stream):
                    self.buffer.logits[:bs] = model.forward()
            if pool is None:
                pool = graph.pool()  # reuse cuda graph handle to reduce memory
            self.graph_map[bs] = graph

        free_memory = get_free_memory(self.device)
        logger.info_rank0(f"Free GPU memory after capturing CUDA graphs: {mem_GB(free_memory)}")

    def can_use_cuda_graph(self, batch: Batch) -> bool:
        return batch.is_decode and batch.size <= self.max_graph_bs

    def replay(self, batch: Batch) -> torch.Tensor:
        assert self.can_use_cuda_graph(batch)
        self.buffer.copy_from(batch)
        g = self.graph_map[batch.padded_size]
        self.attn_backend.prepare_for_replay(batch)
        g.replay()
        return self.buffer.logits[: batch.size]

    def pad_batch(self, batch: Batch) -> None:
        padded_size = (  # choose the first available batch size
            next(bs for bs in self.graph_bs_list if bs >= batch.size)
            if self.can_use_cuda_graph(batch)
            else batch.size
        )
        batch.padded_reqs = batch.reqs + [self.dummy_req] * (padded_size - batch.size)

    # NOTE: This must be called before freeing NCCL resources to prevent program hang
    def destroy_cuda_graphs(self) -> None:
        del self.graph_map
        gc.collect()

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
5	
6	import torch
7	from minisgl.utils import is_sm90_supported, nvtx_annotate
8	
9	if TYPE_CHECKING:
10	    from minisgl.core import Batch
11	
12	
13	@dataclass
14	class BatchSamplingArgs:
15	    temperatures: torch.Tensor | None
16	    top_k: torch.Tensor | None = None
17	    top_p: torch.Tensor | None = None
18	
19	
20	def make_device_tensor(data: List, dtype: torch.dtype, device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
21	    return torch.tensor(data, dtype=dtype, pin_memory=True).to(device, non_blocking=True)
22	
23	
24	def sample_impl(
25	    logits: torch.Tensor,
26	    temperatures: torch.Tensor,
27	    top_k: torch.Tensor | int | None,
28	    top_p: torch.Tensor | float | None,
29	) -> torch.Tensor:
30	    import flashinfer.sampling as sampling
31	
32	    probs = sampling.softmax(logits, temperatures, enable_pdl=is_sm90_supported())
33	    if top_k is None and top_p is None:
34	        return sampling.sampling_from_probs(probs)
35	
36	    if top_p is None:
37	        assert top_k is not None
38	        return sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(probs, top_k)
39	
40	    if top_k is None:
41	        assert top_p is not None
42	        return sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(probs, top_p)
43	
44	    assert top_k is not None and top_p is not None
45	    return sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs(probs, top_k, top_p)
46	
47	
48	@dataclass
49	class Sampler:
50	    device: torch.device
51	    vocab_size: int
52	
53	    def prepare(self, batch: Batch) -> BatchSamplingArgs:
54	        params = [r.sampling_params for r in batch.reqs]
55	        if all(p.is_greedy for p in params):
56	            return BatchSamplingArgs(temperatures=None)
57	
58	        MIN_P = MIN_T = 1e-6
59	        ts = [max(0.0 if p.is_greedy else p.temperature, MIN_T) for p in params]
60	        top_ks = [p.top_k if p.top_k >= 1 else self.vocab_size for p in params]
61	        top_ps = [min(max(p.top_p, MIN_P), 1.0) for p in params]
62	        temperatures = make_device_tensor(ts, torch.float32, self.device)
63	        top_k, top_p = None, None
64	        if any(k != self.vocab_size for k in top_ks):
65	            top_k = make_device_tensor(top_ks, torch.int32, self.device)
66	        if any(p < 1.0 for p in top_ps):
67	            top_p = make_device_tensor(top_ps, torch.float32, self.device)
68	        return BatchSamplingArgs(temperatures, top_k=top_k, top_p=top_p)
69	
70	    @nvtx_annotate("Sampler")
71	    def sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor, args: BatchSamplingArgs) -> torch.Tensor:
72	        with torch.cuda.nvtx.range("Sampler"):
73	            if args.temperatures is None:  # greedy sampling
74	                return torch.argmax(logits, dim=-1)
75	            return sample_impl(logits.float(), args.temperatures, args.top_k, args.top_p)
76

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import argparse
4	import os
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import List, Tuple
7	
8	import torch
9	from minisgl.distributed import DistributedInfo
10	from minisgl.scheduler import SchedulerConfig
11	from minisgl.utils import init_logger
12	
13	
14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
15	class ServerArgs(SchedulerConfig):
16	    server_host: str = "127.0.0.1"
17	    server_port: int = 1919
18	    num_tokenizer: int = 0
19	    silent_output: bool = False
20	
21	    @property
22	    def share_tokenizer(self) -> bool:
23	        return self.num_tokenizer == 0
24	
25	    @property
26	    def zmq_frontend_addr(self) -> str:
27	        return "ipc:///tmp/minisgl_3" + self._unique_suffix
28	
29	    @property
30	    def zmq_tokenizer_addr(self) -> str:
31	        if self.share_tokenizer:
32	            return self.zmq_detokenizer_addr
33	        result = "ipc:///tmp/minisgl_4" + self._unique_suffix
34	        assert result != self.zmq_detokenizer_addr
35	        return result
36	
37	    @property
38	    def tokenizer_create_addr(self) -> bool:
39	        return self.share_tokenizer
40	
41	    @property
42	    def backend_create_detokenizer_link(self) -> bool:
43	        return not self.share_tokenizer
44	
45	    @property
46	    def frontend_create_tokenizer_link(self) -> bool:
47	        return not self.share_tokenizer
48	
49	    @property
50	    def distributed_addr(self) -> str:
51	        return f"tcp://127.0.0.1:{self.server_port + 1}"
52	
53
